articles.title: IND A vs SL A: Why was Super Over played despite fading light? Decision raises eyebrows
articles.description: A tied India A vs Sri Lanka A match ended in controversy after a Super Over was played in near-darkness. Despite deteriorating light, captain Tilak Varma pushed for a result, leading to player struggles and an ugly post-match altercation. Officials' decision to continue under poor visibility remains a major talking point.

articles.title: UK to supply uranium to Ukraine, ramp up sanctions on Russia: Starmer
articles.description: With a strategic move to fortify Ukraine's energy strategy, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that Britain will provide enriched uranium for its nuclear power plants. This pivotal energy partnership, backed by £210 million from UK Export Finance, will ensure two years of energy supply and sustain many jobs across the UK.

articles.title: Watch: Lebanese journalist attacked by Israeli drones while reporting
articles.description: A journalist, Hadi Hoteit, was injured by Israeli drone shrapnel while reporting in Kfar Tebnit, southern Lebanon. Despite clear press markings on his vest, helmet, and car, he was targeted in an open area.

articles.title: Middle East crisis may be over, but how long before Strait of Hormuz goes back to normal?
articles.description: A tentative US-Iran deal signals an end to a 100-day conflict impacting global oil. While prices dipped, analysts warn full recovery of Strait of Hormuz flows will take weeks, possibly months, due to shipping bottlenecks and ongoing mine clearance. Producers await lasting peace assurances before fully resuming output, with full normalization expected gradually.

articles.title: UPSC Prelims Result 2026 PDF Download: 13,343 candidates qualify for Civil Services Mains, check roll number list and next steps
articles.description: UPSC has released the Civil Services Prelims 2026 result in PDF format, allowing candidates to download the roll number list and check their qualification status for the Mains examination. A total of 13,343 candidates have cleared the Preliminary stage against 1,016 vacancies. Qualified candidates must complete mandatory online formalities between June 19 and June 28, 2026. The Civil Services Main Examination will begin on August 21, 2026, marking the next stage of selection.

articles.title: UPSC Prelims Result 2026: Five mistakes qualified candidates should avoid before Mains
articles.description: With the UPSC Prelims 2026 results declared, qualified candidates must avoid common mistakes that could affect their chances in the next stage. From missing the mandatory June 19–28 application window to delaying Mains preparation, neglecting answer-writing practice and overlooking optional subjects, experts warn that small errors can have significant consequences. The article outlines key pitfalls to avoid as 13,343 shortlisted candidates prepare for the Civil Services Main Examination beginning on August 21, 2026.

articles.title: Re-NEET UG 2026: NTA launches portal to report fake paper leak claims, impersonation ahead of June 21 exam
articles.description: Re-NEET UG 2026: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has launched a dedicated portal for reporting suspicious claims and activities related to Re-NEET UG 2026, scheduled for June 21. Candidates can flag fake websites, social media accounts claiming access to question papers, individuals offering leaked papers, and those impersonating NTA officials. The initiative aims to curb misinformation and malpractice following the cancellation of the original NEET UG 2026 exam due to a major paper leak controversy.

articles.title: Richard Ayoade involved in a car crash in South East London
articles.description: Renowned comedian Richard Ayoade was caught in a dramatic car accident in South East London. Eyewitnesses reported that a fast-moving vehicle struck pedestrians before crashing into Ayoade's car. While the beloved actor appeared to be holding his neck, two women who were victims of the event were quickly transported to the hospital.

articles.title: Simran, Silambarasan TR and Shaam meet Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
articles.description: Kollywood stars Simran, Silambarasan TR, and Shaam met with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay, extending warm wishes for his new political journey. The actors shared nostalgic moments and heartfelt messages, celebrating Vijay's transition from cinema to leadership. Their interactions sparked significant fan engagement online, highlighting the strong bonds within the film fraternity.

articles.title: Emma Heming provides a health update about Bruce Willis
articles.description: In a recent update, Emma Heming clarified her previous comments regarding Bruce Willis's health condition. She explained that Frontotemporal Dementia (FTD) predominantly influences his language skills and behavior, while memory remains relatively intact. Emma highlighted that FTD is distinct from Alzheimer's and is frequently seen in younger adults. Bruce is under continuous, professional care in a specialized environment.

articles.title: Arjun Sarja's 'Blast' OTT release date confirmed
articles.description: Arjun Sarja's action drama 'Blast' is set for its OTT release on Netflix on June 25, following a successful theatrical run. The film, which blends action, family emotions, and social themes, features Sarja as a martial arts instructor fighting against a mining mafia. Ravi Basrur's music and Sarja's performance have been lauded.
